items.instruction: Check this box on every SIJS filing because the firm always files a G-28 with the I-360.
items.where_to_verify: Firm Records; G-28; State Bar Records

items.instruction: Bar No. 18826790 for Manuel E. Solis. Use the appearing attorney's bar number if a different attorney signs the G-28.
items.where_to_verify: Firm Records; G-28; State Bar Records

items.instruction: Firm USCIS Online Account No. 087361245002. Use the appearing attorney's individual USCIS account if one is assigned.

Always mark 'Special Immigrant Juvenile' (Item 1.c) on SIJS filings. Leave all other classification boxes (Amerasian, Widow(er), Religious Worker, Iraqi/Afghan Translator, VAWA self-petitioners, etc.) unmarked.
items.where_to_verify: Juvenile Court Order; Predicate Order; Declaration

items.form_text: U.S. Consulate/Embassy City for consular processing (if applicable)
items.instruction: Leave blank when the juvenile is in the United States and will adjust status (typical SIJS case). Complete only if the beneficiary will consular-process abroad.

items.form_text: U.S. Consulate/Embassy Country for consular processing (if applicable)
items.instruction: Same as Item 1.a: blank for stateside cases, country name for consular processing.

items.form_text: Has the person ever been in immigration proceedings (exclusion, deportation, removal, or rescission)?
items.instruction: Determine from the FBI rap sheet, EOIR portal, FOIA, and the case strategy memo. Mark Yes if there is any active or terminated removal/deportation history; otherwise mark No.

items.form_text: Is the person currently in the United States and will apply for adjustment of status if a visa becomes available?
items.instruction: For SIJS this is generally Yes (the juvenile is in the U.S. and intends to file I-485 once a visa is available). Mark No only if consular processing is planned.

excluded_sections.name: Part 4, Items 2.a-2.b, 3, 4.a, 4.b, 7
excluded_sections.reason: Items for non-SIJS classifications or duplicative person data; leave blank in SIJS filings.

items.instruction: Leave every field on this page empty for I-360 SIJS filings.
Leave every field on this page empty for I-360 SIJS filings.
Leave every field on this page empty for I-360 SIJS filings.

items.section: Part 8. Complete Only If Filing for a Special Immigrant Juvenile
items.form_text: SIJ Eligibility - Marital status at time of filing
items.instruction: SIJS requires the juvenile to be unmarried at the time of filing AND through adjudication. Mark Yes only after confirming with the client and reviewing any prior marriage/divorce/annulment records.

options.label: Yes - unmarried at time of filing

items.section: Part 8. Complete Only If Filing for a Special Immigrant Juvenile
items.form_text: SIJ Eligibility - Under 21 years of age at time of filing
items.instruction: SIJS requires the juvenile to be under 21 on the filing date. Confirm by comparing the date of birth to the planned filing date.

options.label: Yes - under 21 at time of filing

items.where_to_verify: Juvenile Court Order; Predicate Order; Declaration

items.section: Part 8. Complete Only If Filing for a Special Immigrant Juvenile
items.form_text: Name of state court and case number issuing the SIJ predicate order
items.instruction: Use the format 'State Court Name - Case No. XXX' (e.g., 'Harris County District Court, 309th Family Division - Case No. 2024-12345').
items.where_to_verify: Juvenile Court Order; Predicate Order; Declaration

excluded_sections.name: Part 8 detailed findings (court order checkboxes Pt8Line3a, 3b, 3A_Checkbox, 3b_Checkbox, 4.a, 4.b, 5, 6.a, 6.b)
excluded_sections.reason: These checkboxes capture the precise legal findings of the state court order (parental reunification not viable, best-interest finding, abuse/neglect/abandonment basis, custody arrangement). The attorney transcribes them directly from the certified court order. They are case-specific and not authored as standardized prompts here; the attorney completes them from the order itself during drafting.

items.section: Part 12. Interpreter's Contact Information, Certification, and Signature
items.instruction: Provide the interpreter's family name. Required only when Part 11, Item 1 was marked 'with interpreter'.

items.section: Part 12. Interpreter's Contact Information, Certification, and Signature
items.instruction: Provide the interpreter's given name. Required only when an interpreter was used.

items.section: Part 12. Interpreter's Contact Information, Certification, and Signature
items.form_text: Interpreter's Business or Organization Name (if any)
items.instruction: Provide the interpreter's firm or organization, when applicable; leave blank for individual interpreters.

items.section: Part 12. Interpreter's Contact Information, Certification, and Signature
items.instruction: Same language as Part 11, Item 1.b.

items.section: Part 13. Contact Information, Declaration, and Signature of Person Preparing this Petition

items.section: Part 13. Contact Information, Declaration, and Signature of Person Preparing this Petition
items.form_text: Preparer's Date of Signature (Mmm DD YYYY)
items.instruction: Date the attorney signs Part 13. Must use 'Mmm DD YYYY' format and match the G-28 attorney signature date when possible.

items.instruction: Use these slots only when an answer above did not fit. Each entry requires: Page Number, Part Number, Item Number, and Additional Information. Provide the juvenile's family name, given name, middle name, and A-Number at the top of the page (overflow anchors).
